report_name: Strategic Education Data Breach Exposes SSNs, Passports of Over 100,000

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Actor behind Strategic Education Breach
threat_actors.description: The unauthorized third party responsible for the data breach at Strategic Education, Inc. in February 2026, which resulted in the exfiltration of Personally Identifiable Information (PII) for over 100,000 individuals.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Information Repositories
attack_patterns.description: The attacker navigated Strategic Education's network to find and access databases or file shares containing student and employee PII.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The attacker exfiltrated files containing sensitive PII, such as SSNs and passport numbers, from the network.

attack_patterns.name: Archive Collected Data
attack_patterns.description: The attacker likely created archive files such as 'students.csv' or 'employees.zip' to stage sensitive bulk data for exfiltration, as suggested by hunting hints.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: The attacker may have used PowerShell with encoded commands to execute payloads or perform actions on the network, as suggested by hunting hints for 'powershell -enc'.

identities.name: Strategic Education, Inc.
identities.description: A prominent US-based education services company that was the victim of a data breach affecting over 100,000 individuals.

identities.name: Strayer University
identities.description: An educational institution operated by Strategic Education, Inc. whose students and staff were affected by the data breach.

identities.name: Capella University
identities.description: An educational institution operated by Strategic Education, Inc. whose students and staff were affected by the data breach.

indicators.name: Suspicious Bulk Data Archive Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Bulk Data Archive Creation
id: 9b1d8c3a-7e4f-4b1a-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the creation of large archive files with names that suggest they contain sensitive bulk data, as seen in the Strategic Education data breach.'
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/strategic-education-june-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1560
  - attack.collection
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\students.csv'
      - '\employees.zip'
      - '\all_students.zip'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of archive files with names suggesting they contain sensitive bulk data like 'students.csv' or 'employees.zip', a TTP for data staging before exfiltration.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: f8a7b6c5-d4e3-4f2a-1b0c-9d8e7f6a5b4c
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the use of PowerShell with the -enc (encoded) flag, a common technique for obfuscating malicious commands.'
references:
  - https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/strategic-education-june-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-06
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with encoded commands, a common obfuscation technique used by attackers.
